mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in 60% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly drive positive equity market momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side constraints that immediately trigger negative short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns at a statistically significant rate.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in the majority of months where CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal income growth reveals underlying demand fragility, causing negative equity market adjustments over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal does not predict negative 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth outpaces RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market normalization, which sustains positive equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines during recovery phases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns at a rate exceeding 55%.
